navigation_overview:
The top navigation bar appears on every page and contains the site name "MoodleTest" on the left, which links to the home page, followed by links for Home, Dashboard, and My Courses. On the right side, a notifications bell icon shows alerts when clicked, a messaging icon opens the messaging drawer, and the user's initials in a circular icon opens the user menu dropdown. The user menu contains links for Profile, Grades, Calendar, Private Files, Reports, and Preferences, followed by a Log out option that ends the session and returns to the login page. When viewing a course, a horizontal tab bar appears below the course title with tabs for Course, Participants, Grades, Activities, and Competencies. Teachers also see a Settings tab that is not visible to students. When viewing an activity, breadcrumbs at the top show the full navigation path from the course, with each segment as a clickable link. The Course Index sidebar on the left side of course pages provides a hierarchical tree of all sections and activities, with expand/collapse arrows per section and a close button (X) to hide the sidebar. The currently active item is highlighted.

---

nodes.title: Course Edit Mode and Activity Chooser

module_summaries.summary:
Line 1: Users can authenticate by entering Username and Password, access the site as a guest, and view cookie information.
Line 2: Shows login form fields, inline error messages for invalid/empty credentials, guest access and cookies notice, and disabled lost-password link.

Line 1: Users can view and filter upcoming teaching activities, change timeline range/sort, search activities, switch courses, and create new events.
Line 2: Displays a personalized greeting, a Timeline of upcoming actions (or an empty state), time-range and sort controls, search field, and a monthly Calendar view with course filter.

Line 1: Users can toggle Edit mode, add new dashboard blocks, and reset the page to the default layout.
Line 2: Shows Edit mode controls and a list of available block types to add, plus the Reset page option and Add a block entry point.

Line 1: Users can browse, search, filter and sort their courses, change the layout view, star courses, and remove courses from view.
Line 2: Displays course cards with banner, clickable course name, category, and a three-dot menu offering Star and Remove options.

Line 1: Users can navigate a course by expanding and collapsing sections and opening activities and resources.
Line 2: Displays the course name, navigation tabs, collapsible sections with section names, and lists of activities/resources with type icons and clickable names.

module_summaries.module_title: Course Edit Mode and Activity Chooser
module_summaries.summary:
Line 1: Users can enable Edit mode to rename, edit, duplicate, hide, move or delete sections and activities, perform bulk actions, and add activities/resources.
Line 2: Shows inline edit controls, section- and activity-level three-dot menus with authoring actions, bulk actions toolbar, and Add an activity/resource buttons.

Line 1: Users can create and configure assignments by entering a required name, adding descriptions/files, setting availability windows with enable toggles, and choosing submission/feedback options.
Line 2: Displays the Assignment name field, collapsible panels (General, Availability, etc.), description editor, additional files area, date/time pickers with enable toggles, and submission/feedback settings.

Line 1: Users can edit course settings including names, category, visibility, dates, summary, image, format and related layout/appearance options.
Line 2: Displays collapsible panels with Course full and short names, category and visibility dropdowns, start/end dates (with enable toggle), course summary editor, image upload, and format/layout controls.

Line 1: Users can view and filter enrolled users, apply complex filter conditions, use alphabetical filters, and open the enrol users dialog to add participants.
Line 2: Displays the enrolled-users scope dropdown, filter builder (Any toggle, attribute selector, added conditions), Clear/Apply buttons, alphabetical name filters, and a list of enrolled users with checkboxes.

Line 1: Teachers can open the grading interface for individual students and navigate assignment settings and reports via the tab bar.
Line 2: Displays assignment metadata (Opened date, Due date), full description with attachments, a Grade button, and a Grading summary panel showing participants, submissions, needs grading, visibility and time remaining.

Line 1: Users can search, filter and inspect student submission records, preview online text, download submitted files, and navigate to student profiles.
Line 2: Displays a table of submissions with student identity, submission status, grading status, submission timestamps, time since submission, online text preview and file submission links.

Line 1: Users can view grades in a spreadsheet-style report, switch report types, search and filter students/groups, and access per-column and per-cell actions.
Line 2: Displays a grade table with activities as columns and students as rows, report-type selector, user search/filters, per-column action menus and per-cell menus for grade actions.

Line 1: Users can view their profile details and initiate messaging or navigate to edit profile and related reports/links.
Line 2: Displays the user's initials icon, full name, message button, profile description, contact details (email, timezone), links to associated course profiles, data retention and report links.

Line 1: Users can edit their personal information, change email visibility, upload or change their profile picture, and expand/collapse profile sections.
Line 2: Displays editable fields for first/last name, email, email visibility, MoodleNet ID, city/country, timezone, description editor, current user picture and upload area, and Additional names fields.

Line 1: Users can terminate their authenticated session by selecting Log out, which returns them to the login page.
Line 2: Performs logout and redirect; displays the login page after session termination and prevents access to protected pages without re-authentication.

test_cases.title: Prevent saving when an edited grade is outside configured range
test_cases.workflow: Save changes to apply inline edits when Edit mode is enabled
test_cases.preconditions: User is a teacher and the Grader report page is open.
steps: Click "Edit mode" to enable editing
Enter an out-of-range value into a grade cell
Click "Save changes"
test_cases.expected_result: Saving is blocked, the out-of-range cell shows inline validation, and no edits are applied.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Values outside the configured grade range block saving

test_cases.title: Cancel a per-cell grade edit leaves original grade unchanged
test_cases.workflow: Edit individual grade entries via per-cell three-dot menu
test_cases.preconditions: Grader report page is open and the grade table with per-cell three-dot menus is visible
steps: Click the per-cell three-dot menu for the target student's activity cell
Click "Edit" in the per-cell menu
Modify the grade value
Click "Cancel" or close the edit without saving
test_cases.expected_result: The grade cell retains the original value and no changes are applied.

test_cases.title: Search for non-matching student name shows no rows
test_cases.workflow: Narrow rows by student name or group using User search and filter controls
test_cases.preconditions: Grader report is open and grade table contains enrolled students.
steps: Enter a student name that does not match any enrolled student into the User search field
Trigger the search control (e.g., press Enter or click search)
test_cases.expected_result: Grade table shows no student rows and displays an empty-state or no-results indicator.
test_cases.spec_evidence: User search and filter controls narrow rows by student name or group

test_cases.title: Save grade settings with all required fields empty
test_cases.workflow: Edit an activity's grade settings via per-column action menu
test_cases.preconditions: Grader report is open and user has teacher privileges.
steps: Open the per-column action menu in the target activity's column header
Click "Edit grade settings"
Leave all required fields empty
Click "Save" or "Submit"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Per-column action menu provides options to edit that activity's grade settings

test_cases.title: Entering an out-of-range grade via per-cell edit shows inline flag
test_cases.workflow: Edit individual grade entries via per-cell three-dot menu
test_cases.preconditions: Grader report page is open and the grade table with per-cell three-dot menus is visible
steps: Click the per-cell three-dot menu for the target student's activity cell
Click "Edit" in the per-cell menu
Fill the grade value with a value outside the configured grade range
Click "Save" or confirm the edit
test_cases.expected_result: The grade cell shows the entered value and displays an inline flag indicating the value is outside the allowed range.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Values outside the configured grade range are flagged inline

test_cases.title: Multiple edits including an out-of-range value block saving
test_cases.workflow: Save changes to apply inline edits when Edit mode is enabled
test_cases.preconditions: User is a teacher and the Grader report page is open.
steps: Click "Edit mode" to enable editing
Edit several grade cells so at least one contains an out-of-range value
Click "Save changes"
test_cases.expected_result: Save is blocked, inline flags appear for the invalid cells, and none of the edits are applied.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Values outside the configured grade range block saving

test_cases.title: Editing is not allowed when Edit mode is disabled
test_cases.workflow: Save changes to apply inline edits when Edit mode is enabled
test_cases.preconditions: User is a teacher and the Grader report page is open.
steps: Ensure "Edit mode" is disabled
Attempt to modify a grade cell
test_cases.expected_result: Grade cells are not editable when Edit mode is disabled.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Grade cells become editable inline when Edit mode is enabled

test_cases.title: Canceling an edit does not persist grade settings changes
test_cases.workflow: Edit an activity's grade settings via per-column action menu
test_cases.preconditions: Grader report is open and user has teacher privileges.
steps: Open the per-column action menu in the target activity's column header
Click "Edit grade settings"
Modify one or more editable settings in the grade settings form
Click "Cancel" or close the settings dialog
Re-open "Edit grade settings" for the same activity
test_cases.expected_result: Previously saved settings remain unchanged and the unsubmitted modifications are not persisted.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Per-column action menu provides options to edit that activity's grade settings

test_cases.title: Flag out-of-range values inline when editing
test_cases.workflow: Save changes to apply inline edits when Edit mode is enabled
test_cases.preconditions: User is a teacher and the Grader report page is open.
steps: Click "Edit mode" to enable editing
Enter an out-of-range value into a grade cell
test_cases.expected_result: The edited cell is visibly flagged inline to indicate the value is outside the configured range.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Values outside the configured grade range are flagged inline

test_cases.title: Apply group filter for a group with no enrolled students results in empty table
test_cases.workflow: Narrow rows by student name or group using User search and filter controls
test_cases.preconditions: Grader report is open and at least one group exists that contains no enrolled students.
steps: Open the group filter control and select the group that has no enrolled students
Apply the group filter if an explicit apply action is required
test_cases.expected_result: Grade table shows no student rows and displays an empty-state or no-results indicator.
test_cases.spec_evidence: User search and filter controls narrow rows by student name or group

test_cases.title: Profile page displays all core profile elements
test_cases.workflow: Main workflow
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Profile page is open
steps: Verify the following elements are visible on the Profile page: teacher's circular initials icon, full name, Message button, email address, visibility note, timezone, Edit profile link, Data retention summary link, links to associated course profiles, links to Blog entries, Forum posts, Forum discussions, Learning plans, Browser sessions link, Grades overview link, and Login activity showing First and Last access with exact dates and relative time indicators
test_cases.expected_result: All listed profile elements are visible and correctly labeled.

test_cases.title: Edit profile updates and persists profile description
test_cases.workflow: Main workflow
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Profile page is open
steps: Click the Edit profile link
Modify the profile description field and any other editable user detail
Click Save (or equivalent) to persist changes
test_cases.expected_result: Profile page reflects the updated profile description and saved details.

test_cases.title: Open an associated course profile (triggerable from: any course link)
test_cases.workflow: Main workflow
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Profile page is open
steps: Click any course link under the Course details section
Verify the destination shows the course profile or course title/header
test_cases.expected_result: The selected associated course profile opens and displays the expected course identifier or title.

test_cases.title: Login activity shows exact dates and relative time indicators
test_cases.workflow: Main workflow
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Profile page is open
steps: Locate the Login activity section
Verify that First access and Last access entries display exact date values and also show relative time indicators (e.g., 'x days ago')
test_cases.expected_result: Login activity lists First and Last access with both exact dates and relative time indicators.

test_cases.title: Send a message to the teacher via Message button
test_cases.workflow: Main workflow
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Profile page is open
steps: Click the Message button
Compose a message in the messaging drawer and click Send
test_cases.expected_result: Message is sent and a confirmation or the sent message appears in the conversation thread.

test_cases.title: Access Data retention summary from Privacy and policies
test_cases.workflow: Main workflow
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Profile page is open
steps: Click the Data retention summary link in the Privacy and policies section
Verify the Data retention summary content or heading is displayed
test_cases.expected_result: Data retention summary content or page is displayed.

test_cases.title: Open Miscellaneous links: Blog, Forum posts, Forum discussions, and Learning plans
test_cases.workflow: Main workflow
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Profile page is open
steps: Click each link under the Miscellaneous section (Blog entries, Forum posts, Forum discussions, Learning plans) one at a time
Verify each link opens the corresponding list or page for that content type
test_cases.expected_result: Each Miscellaneous link navigates to and displays the appropriate list or page.

test_cases.title: Access Reports links for Browser sessions and Grades overview
test_cases.workflow: Main workflow
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Profile page is open
steps: Click the Browser sessions link and verify browser session information or report is displayed
Click the Grades overview link and verify the grades overview is displayed
test_cases.expected_result: Browser sessions and Grades overview pages or reports are displayed when their links are clicked.

test_cases.title: Login activity entries include First and Last access to site values
test_cases.workflow: Main workflow
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Profile page is open
steps: Locate the Login activity section
Verify that both First and Last access to site are shown with exact dates
test_cases.expected_result: Both First and Last access to site are displayed with exact date values.
test_cases.spec_evidence: First and Last access to site

test_cases.title: Profile renders correctly when description is not provided
test_cases.workflow: Main workflow
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Profile page is open for a teacher without a profile description
steps: Confirm the profile description area is empty or absent
Verify other profile elements (initials icon, full name, Message button, user details, and links) are still visible and the page layout remains consistent
test_cases.expected_result: Profile displays correctly without a profile description; other elements are present and layout is unaffected.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Profile description is optional

test_cases.title: Update profile with valid required fields and new picture
test_cases.workflow: Update profile validates all required fields and upload constraints before saving and refreshes the profile page on success
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Edit Profile page
steps: Fill all required fields (First name, Last name, Email address) and other editable fields to change (City/town, Country, Timezone, Description)
Upload a valid new picture via the new picture upload area (drag-and-drop or file select) and fill Picture description if applicable
Click "Update profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile page refreshes and displays the updated field values and the new user picture.

test_cases.title: Update profile saving optional fields without changing picture
test_cases.workflow: Update profile validates all required fields and upload constraints before saving and refreshes the profile page on success
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Edit Profile page
steps: Fill all required fields (First name, Last name, Email address) and fill optional fields (Additional names, Interests, any site-defined Optional custom fields)
Click "Update profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile page refreshes and displays the saved optional field values.

test_cases.title: Drag-and-drop picture upload updates picture and refreshes profile page
test_cases.workflow: Update profile validates all required fields and upload constraints before saving and refreshes the profile page on success

steps: Drag-and-drop a valid image into the new picture upload area
Click "Update profile"
test_cases.expected_result: Profile page refreshes and the new picture is displayed as the user picture.

Click "Cancel"
test_cases.expected_result: No edits are saved; profile displays the original values and changes are discarded.

test_cases.title: Cancel after selecting a new user picture (drag-and-drop)

steps: Upload a new picture using the new picture upload area (drag-and-drop or file select) and fill the Picture description field
Click "Cancel"
test_cases.expected_result: Original user picture and picture description remain; the uploaded picture is not saved.

test_cases.title: User picture area displays current picture on Edit Profile page
test_cases.workflow: Update profile validates all required fields and upload constraints before saving and refreshes the profile page on success
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Edit Profile page
steps: Inspect the User picture area
test_cases.expected_result: The current user picture is visible in the User picture area.

test_cases.title: Cancel after changing the Description rich text

test_cases.preconditions: Edit Profile form is open and the current Description is visible
steps: Edit the Description using the rich text editor

test_cases.title: Cancel after expanding and modifying optional and custom fields

test_cases.title: Log out terminates session and redirects to login page
test_cases.workflow: Selecting Log out terminates the current authenticated session and redirects to the login page
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and the top navigation bar is visible
steps: Click the user's initials icon in the top navigation
Click "Log out" in the user menu
test_cases.expected_result: The current authenticated session is terminated and the application redirects to the login page.

test_cases.title: Access to protected pages requires re-authentication after logout (triggerable via browser Back or direct link)
test_cases.workflow: Selecting Log out terminates the current authenticated session and redirects to the login page
test_cases.preconditions: User is authenticated and a protected page is open
steps: Click the user's initials icon in the top navigation
Click "Log out" in the user menu
Use the browser Back button to return to the previous (protected) page or attempt to open a protected link such as "Dashboard"
test_cases.expected_result: Access to the protected page is blocked and the login page or re-authentication prompt is shown; protected content is not accessible without signing in.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Access to all protected pages requires re-authentication after logout
